company_type: Professional Services - Risk Capital and Human Capital Solutions

dependencies: Reliance on third parties to perform key functions including technology, information security, funds transfers, data processing, support functions, and administration

Dependence on correspondents, agents and other brokerage intermediaries, insurance markets, data providers, plan trustees, payroll service providers, benefits administrators, software and system vendors, business process outsourcing providers
risks: Third parties may act in ways that could harm business
Failure by third parties to comply with service level agreements or regulatory requirements
Disruptions in service or cessation of service by any service provider due to systems failures, cybersecurity or data privacy incidents, capacity constraints, financial difficulties
Reliance on complex information technology systems and networks vulnerable to cyber-attacks, data breaches, and unauthorized access
Dependence on third-party vendors for technology systems with potential breakdowns or security breaches

regulatory_risks: Extensive governmental regulation in jurisdictions worldwide including Irish Companies Act, U.S. securities laws, FCA rules and regulations
Licensing requirements and regulation as insurance producers, brokers, agents, consultants, reinsurance brokers, or managing general agents
Investment of fiduciary funds subject to regulatory requirements and limits on types of investments
Pension and financial laws including oversight by FCA in U.K., SEC and DOL in U.S.
Investment advisory services regulated by SEC, DOL, FINRA, and state authorities
Conflict of interest regulations and anti-trust laws related to Market Derived Income (MDI)
Data privacy and protection regulations including General Data Protection Regulation in E.U., evolving privacy laws in China, Brazil, and U.S. jurisdictions
Schrems II case impacts on data export from E.U.
New regulations on automated decision making and artificial intelligence
ESG reporting, diligence and disclosure rules and regulations
Climate-related disclosure requirements
Competition authority scrutiny and potential enforcement actions
Anti-money laundering, wage-and-hour standards, employment and labor relations regulations
Anti-corruption laws including Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (U.S.) and Bribery Act of 2010 (U.K.)
Trade sanctions laws, restrictions and export controls
Currency and reserve requirements in certain countries
Health insurance industry changes and potential legislative changes affecting health care exchange business
Regulations affecting viability, value, use or delivery of benefits and human resources programs
Changes in laws governing health and welfare plans, defined contribution plans, or defined benefit plans
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax regime (15% country-by-country minimum tax)
Potential changes in tax laws including U.S. Inflation Reduction Act (1% excise tax on stock redemptions)
Foreign Account Tax Compliance provisions of the Hiring Incentives to Restore Employment Act

sanction_risks: Trade sanctions laws, restrictions and export controls may impact operations
Geopolitical conflicts including ongoing Russian war in Ukraine and conflicts in the Middle East
International risks associated with global operations including impacts from military conflicts or political instability
Potential governmental actions affecting flow of goods, services, and currency in emerging markets
